title: zornia contorta
intro: Zornia contorta, also known as wild madder or stickseed, is a perennial plant native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The plant is rich in flavonoids and alkaloids, which have been found to have various health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-cancer, and anti-diabetic properties. Herbal preparations of Zornia contorta can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and have been used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions.
